summary: Audience recording of a mixed‑quality 1993 Chinese New Year show with standout Space jam and smoky Eyes→Estimated→Terrapin, but uneven sound and vocal issues.
review: The performance features a memorable second set highlighted by the Eyes > Estimated > Terrapin sequence and a thrilling Carlos Santana‑joined Space, while the first set is described as mellow and slower. Recording quality is contested – one reviewer calls it an excellent AUD capture, another finds it low‑volume and poorly mixed, leaving the overall listening experience uneven.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is praised for several jams and guest appearance (70% weight) but detracted by vocal problems and inconsistent energy; recording quality splits between excellent and poor (30% weight), yielding a mid‑range overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: One reviewer hails it as one of the best AUD recordings, while another reports low volume, muffled mix and barely audible bass; separation between instruments is limited.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Opening act of Chinese acrobats set a festive tone, the first set is described as mellow and slow, the second set builds momentum with strong Drums and a guest‑filled Space, but vocal fatigue and uneven pacing leave parts of the show feeling under‑cooked.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised by some for his tone and energy; others feel his playing meanders without direction.
band_member_comments.Bob: Multiple reviewers note a hoarse, weak vocal performance during this period.
band_member_comments.Drums: Consistently praised – the Drums segment is called strong and a highlight of the show.
